must_fix_in_this_iterate.title: Fallback gate too strict — fire artifact path on cs_alerts is None alone, not (cs AND db) is None
must_fix_in_this_iterate.rationale: Dependabot is free and commonly enabled on private repos without GHAS; only Code Scanning (SARIF Upload) is gated by GHAS. The original gate (`cs AND db both None`) would skip the artifact path whenever Dependabot is enabled even though the SAST source is missing. The artifact is the canonical SAST replacement; fire on cs_alerts is None.
must_fix_in_this_iterate.applied: Artifact path fires when `cs_alerts is None`. The action-unit's detail renders per-source counts independently (code-scanning / dependabot / shipwright-security each as their own line) so the operator sees which sources contributed.

must_fix_in_this_iterate.title: Recency / freshness — no max-age check on artifact run
must_fix_in_this_iterate.rationale: A dormant workflow's last successful run could be months old; ingesting that as 'current' would surface stale vulnerabilities or auto-resolve real ones based on a stale clean scan.
must_fix_in_this_iterate.applied: Introduce `SHIPWRIGHT_GITHUB_ARTIFACT_MAX_AGE_DAYS` env var (default 14). `latest_security_workflow_run` returns None when the candidate run's `created_at` is older than the cutoff. Auto-resolve is gated on freshness — a stale clean run never resolves open items.

must_fix_in_this_iterate.title: Branch filtering and discovery — pick default-branch runs only
must_fix_in_this_iterate.rationale: Without branch filter the importer could pick a feature-branch run; without explicit default-branch discovery the helper contract is ambiguous.
must_fix_in_this_iterate.applied: `latest_security_workflow_run` filters via `gh api ... runs?branch=<default>&status=success&per_page=10`. The branch is auto-resolved by the existing `github_api.default_branch()` helper — callers do NOT supply it manually.

must_fix_in_this_iterate.title: Source-switching dedup risk — explicit tests for cross-source transitions
must_fix_in_this_iterate.rationale: Same dedup key across API and artifact paths is by design (one item per repo), but transitions can re-emit with different detail/launchPayload. Need empirical tests covering all transition orderings.
must_fix_in_this_iterate.applied: Test `test_github_triage_artifact_fallback.py` adds 4 transition cases: (a) artifact emit → later GHAS comes online → second import idempotent on existing item, (b) GHAS emit → later GHAS goes offline + artifact succeeds → second import idempotent, (c) artifact clean scan → later GHAS findings → auto-resolve precedes new emit, (d) GHAS clean → later artifact findings → auto-resolve precedes new emit. The `launchPayload` stays frozen at first append per the existing contract.

must_fix_in_this_iterate.title: Derive counts from findings list, not the aggregate
must_fix_in_this_iterate.rationale: `total_findings` and `by_severity` are aggregate counters that could disagree with the `findings` array. Trust the SSoT (the list), not the redundant aggregate.
must_fix_in_this_iterate.applied: Mapper iterates `findings[]` and derives severity counts from each item's `severity` field. `by_severity` aggregate is ignored. Unknown severity values fall back to 'medium' (matching the existing `triage_severity` helper). A non-list `findings` value returns None (treated as malformed).

must_fix_in_this_iterate.title: Subprocess argv hygiene
must_fix_in_this_iterate.rationale: Command injection risk if owner/repo/branch values are interpolated into shell strings.
must_fix_in_this_iterate.applied: All `gh` calls use the existing `_gh_api()` helper or follow the same pattern (argv list, no shell=True, all dynamic values pass through `urllib.parse.quote`/`shlex.quote` where applicable). The new `download_security_findings` passes the integer `run_id` as a list element and uses `tempfile.mkdtemp` for the destination.

must_fix_in_this_iterate.title: Treat artifact contents as untrusted
must_fix_in_this_iterate.rationale: Even though the artifact is from CI, the inputs to that CI (PR bodies, commit messages, scanner rule descriptions) could carry markdown / control chars that render badly downstream.
must_fix_in_this_iterate.applied: The action-unit `detail` and `launchPayload` contain ONLY aggregated counts, a CWE summary count, the workflow run URL, and static text. No raw finding `description` / `rule` strings are rendered. A defensive length cap (1KB) is applied to the assembled detail line.

must_fix_in_this_iterate.title: Robust file discovery after artifact download
must_fix_in_this_iterate.rationale: `gh run download` *usually* flattens single-artifact downloads but a future change or unusual zip layout could nest the file.
must_fix_in_this_iterate.applied: After `gh run download`, the helper does `Path(tmp).rglob('findings.json')` and takes the first match. None if no match.

must_fix_in_this_iterate.title: Search for remaining triage-inbox.md references before deletion
must_fix_in_this_iterate.rationale: Documentation deletes that miss callers create broken links.
must_fix_in_this_iterate.applied: Grep `triage-inbox.md` repo-wide before the delete; remove or rewrite every match.

deferred_with_rationale.title: Drop gh CLI in favor of REST + in-memory zipfile
deferred_with_rationale.rationale_for_deferral: The entire existing `github_api.py` module uses `gh api`. Consistency with the established pattern outweighs the marginal robustness gain. Adopting REST would also require token authentication handling (PAT or GITHUB_TOKEN) which `gh` abstracts. Documented `gh` as a prerequisite in security-ci-setup.md (covers openai-3 too).
deferred_with_rationale.follow_up: If `gh` dependency becomes a deployment blocker (Docker minimal images, etc.), file a follow-up iterate to add a REST fallback inside `_gh_api`.

deferred_with_rationale.title: Workflow identification only by path
deferred_with_rationale.rationale_for_deferral: The path is locked by `shared/scripts/lib/security_workflow.py::WORKFLOW_PATH` — already a SSoT constant. /shipwright-adopt writes the workflow to that exact path. Documenting the constraint in security-ci-setup.md is sufficient for v1.
deferred_with_rationale.follow_up: If user demand for custom workflow paths appears, add a `triage.github_security_workflow_path` config key with the SSoT constant as default.

deferred_with_rationale.title: by_source consumer audit
deferred_with_rationale.rationale_for_deferral: Grep shows `by_source` is currently consumed only by `github_triage.py`'s own callers (the SessionStart hook prints a count summary, the tests assert on it). The new `gh-security:artifact` key is purely additive — no enumerator pattern that would break.
deferred_with_rationale.follow_up: Documented in the iterate ADR; if the WebUI dashboard ever surfaces `by_source` keys, this is the place to revisit.

addressed_summary: 9 findings applied as plan revisions (HIGH: 3, MEDIUM: 5, LOW: 1). 3 findings deferred with documented rationale (MEDIUM: 2, LOW: 1). The HIGH gate-logic bug from both reviewers fundamentally reshaped the architecture: the artifact is now a third parallel source alongside cs_alerts and db_alerts rather than a binary fallback for both.
